hook: Seven federal employees, independently, reported orbs launching other orbs.
A “super-hot” orb hovered over a military facility — then outran the helicopter chasing it, for 20 miles.
Astronauts Borman and Lovell radioed Houston about a “bogey” — the file even has handwritten notes titled “UFO Sighting by Borman.”
The actual air-to-ground audio: Frank Borman reporting his “bogey” to mission control, December 5, 1965.
The complete FBI flying-disc case file, 1947–1968 — incident accounts, photos from Oak Ridge, and proposals on UFO propulsion.
A 747 crew at 41,000 feet photographed a “bright light of enormous intensity” doing corkscrews and 90-degree turns — with contrails too high for any ordinary aircraft.
An FBI Lab sketch over a real site photo: a bronze, ellipsoid object “materializing out of a bright light” — then gone instantly.
Mexico’s Congress heard testimony on a law to formally acknowledge alien life — complete with “two alleged alien corpses.”
An Apollo 17 photo shows three dots in a triangular formation — new US analysis says it could be a “physical object in the scene.”
A UAP skimming just above the ocean, making multiple 90-degree turns at an estimated 80 mph.
